title: Parastrephia teretiuscula
taxonomy: Parastrephia teretiuscula is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants. More specifically, it is placed within the subfamily Mutisioideae, which is a distinct group within the daisy family. The plant's classification is also supported by molecular phylogenetic studies, which have confirmed its placement within this family. Its classification as a member of the Asteraceae family is widely accepted by botanists and taxonomists.

intro: Parastrephia teretiuscula, commonly known as "yerba de Bolivar" or "Parastrephia," is a shrubby, perennial plant native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, offering potential health benefits in treating wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Parastrephia teretiuscula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
